title: Suzuka F1: Ferrari’s double-edged weapon explained 🚨 #Ferrari #SF26 #F1 #JapaneseGP #Mercedes #race
description:
Suzuka is always one of the toughest and most technical tracks in Formula 1, but this year it’s also a major test of energy management—and that could shape the entire race weekend.

Coming into Japan, Mercedes looks like the clear favorite. After a strong performance in China, they appear to have the most complete package on the grid. Suzuka suits them perfectly, especially with their top speed and excellent battery management. With the FIA limiting the use of active aerodynamics, energy deploym

title: Mercedes Controversial Two Phase Front Wing Explained : 2026 F1 FIA Loophole Exposed?
description:
-Mercedes Controversial Two-Phase Front Wing Explained: How They Outsmarted the FIA 400ms Rule (F1 2026 W17)
In the Chinese Grand Prix, Mercedes dropped a clever (or cheeky?) aerodynamic trick on their W17 that has the entire paddock talking — and Ferrari demanding answers from the FIA.
The 2026 F1 regulations strictly limit the front wing flap transition between Straight Mode and Corner Mode to a maximum of 400 milliseconds. But Mercedes appears to have split the movement into two phases:

title: Verstappen And Red Bull’s Massive Problems Explained | F1 Explainer
description:
What has happened to Red Bull Racing and McLaren? The teams that once dominated Formula 1 now find themselves in deep trouble. In China, everything went wrong: Max Verstappen had issues and retired, Isack Hadjar spun, the RB22 lacks both speed and reliability, and McLaren did not even make it to the start. Meanwhile, Mercedes and Ferrari simply drove away from the field.

In this video, we analyse the major technical problems facing Red Bull and McLaren. From Max Verstappen’s disastrous weekend

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed. title: 2026 F1 Australian GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: The factory Mercedes team - George Russell heading Kimi Antonelli - duly finished one-two in Melbourne on Sunday, exhibiting more power, more downforce, more grip and more balance than all of their opposition - and capped all of that with perfect strategy calls.
